stem: Solve \( |[[a]]x - [[b]]| = [[c]] \). Which of the following is a solution?
solution: Removing the absolute value gives \( [[a]]x - [[b]] = \pm [[c]] \), so \( x = \dfrac{[[b]] \pm [[c]]}{[[a]]} \). One solution is \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the value where the inside of the absolute value is zero, not where it equals \( \pm[[c]] \).
This adds \( [[c]] \) to \( [[b]] \) but forgets to divide by the coefficient \( [[a]] \).
This subtracts \( [[c]] \) from \( [[b]] \) but forgets to divide by the coefficient \( [[a]] \).
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by two from a correct solution.
This is off by two from a correct solution.

stem: The solution set of \( |[[a]]x - ([[b]])| < [[c]] \) is an open interval \( (p, q) \). What is \( q - p \), the length of that interval?
solution: The inequality \( |[[a]]x - [[b]]| < [[c]] \) means \( [[b]] - [[c]] < [[a]]x < [[b]] + [[c]] \), so \( \dfrac{[[b]]-[[c]]}{[[a]]} < x < \dfrac{[[b]]+[[c]]}{[[a]]} \). The interval length is \( q - p = \dfrac{2\cdot[[c]]}{[[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 that comes from having two endpoints a distance \( [[c]] \) apart.
This forgets to divide by the coefficient \( [[a]] \) when converting from the \( [[a]]x \) term to \( x \).
This confuses the radius \( [[c]] \) with the center \( [[b]] \) of the interval.
This divides by an extra, unneeded factor of 2.
This is off by one from the correct length.
This is off by one from the correct length.

solution: This equation says \( x \) is equidistant from \( [[a]] \) and \( [[b]] \), so \( x \) is their midpoint: \( x = \dfrac{[[a]] + [[b]]}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is one of the two fixed points, not the midpoint between them.
This is the other fixed point, not the midpoint between them.
This forgets to divide the sum by 2 to get the midpoint.
This subtracts the two fixed points instead of averaging them.
This is off by one from the correct midpoint.
This is off by one from the correct midpoint.

solution: The inequality holds for \( \dfrac{[[b]]-[[c]]}{[[a]]} \le x \le \dfrac{[[b]]+[[c]]}{[[a]]} \). Counting the integers in this closed interval gives \( \left\lfloor \dfrac{[[b]]+[[c]]}{[[a]]} \right\rfloor - \left\lceil \dfrac{[[b]]-[[c]]}{[[a]]} \right\rceil + 1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This floors the raw (un-rounded) interval length \( 2c/a \) and forgets the endpoint-rounding and the +1 needed to count integers.
This treats the coefficient \( [[a]] \) as if it were 1, ignoring that \( x \) is scaled.
This reports the bound \( [[c]] \) itself rather than the count of integers satisfying the inequality.
This reports the center \( [[b]] \) of the interval rather than the count of integers in it.
This is off by one from the correct count.
This is off by one from the correct count.
This is off by two from the correct count.

stem: Which of the following is a solution of \( |[[a]]x + ([[b]])| = [[c]]x + ([[d]]) \)?
solution: Split into the two cases \( [[a]]x + [[b]] = [[c]]x + [[d]] \) and \( [[a]]x + [[b]] = -([[c]]x + [[d]]) \), solve each linear equation, and keep only the roots for which the right side \( [[c]]x + [[d]] \) is non-negative. This gives \( x = [[answer]] \) as a valid solution.
distractor_rationales: This is where the expression inside the absolute value is zero, not a solution of the full equation.
This is where the right-hand side is zero, not a solution of the full equation.
This solves the case-1 linear equation but forgets to check that the right side stays non-negative there.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by two from a correct solution.
This is off by two from a correct solution.

stem: The graph of \( y = |[[a]]x - ([[b]])| \) intersects the horizontal line \( y = [[c]] \) at two points with \( x \)-coordinates \( p \) and \( q \). What is \( p + q \)?
solution: The intersections satisfy \( [[a]]x - [[b]] = \pm [[c]] \), i.e. \( x = \dfrac{[[b]]+[[c]]}{[[a]]} \) and \( x = \dfrac{[[b]]-[[c]]}{[[a]]} \). Their sum is \( p+q = \dfrac{2[[b]]}{[[a]]} = [[answer]] \) (the two \( \pm[[c]] \) terms cancel, leaving the V's axis of symmetry doubled).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 and reports only the axis of symmetry, not the sum of both roots.
This uses \( [[c]] \) (the height of the line) instead of \( [[b]] \) (the vertex location) in the sum formula.
This forgets to divide by the coefficient \( [[a]] \).
This divides by an extra, unneeded factor of 2.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
